rubric_items.reasoning: Both deployments confirmed on rayproject/ray:2.9.0 with full readiness (ray-head 1/1, ray-worker 2/2), oracle passed, and the agent went beyond minimal checks by verifying GCS port 6379 reachability and running `ray status` on the new head pod showing all 3 nodes reconnected. This is a genuine, durable fix, not a lucky pass — it would survive a restart or re-check.

The visible sequence is coherent: perform the image update, watch rollout events to confirm old ReplicaSets scaled down and new ones came up cleanly, re-confirm pod status, then run functional health checks (network connectivity, ray status, final image/ready columns) before reporting done. No backtracking, contradictory actions, or wasted exploration — the extra verification steps are purposeful confirmation, not detours.

Used the exact suggested least-privilege commands (`kubectl set image` per deployment) plus scoped read-only verification (`get events`, `get pods -o wide`, `get deploy -o custom-columns`, a targeted `exec` for a socket connectivity check and `ray status`). No broad or destructive operations; tooling matches task scope precisely.

K8s activity shows only 2 mutations total — exactly the two required `set image` calls for ray-head and ray-worker — with the remaining 29 calls being read-only verification. No collateral changes or unnecessary mutations; the change is as minimal and focused as the task requires.
